description: delegation whose scopes list contains a string that does not parse per SPEC §7.1 grammar — verifier MUST reject with E_BAD_SCOPE_GRAMMAR per §8.1 step 4

expected.rejection_reason: The scope string `lock:seal(recipient bc1qalice…)` is missing the `=` (or `<=`, `<`, etc.) operator between the constraint key and value. Per §7.1, `<constraint>` ::= `<key> <op> <value>`, where `<op>` is one of `=`, `!=`, `<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`, `*`. A space alone is not a valid operator. Conformant verifiers MUST refuse to compute an `id` for this delegation and MUST NOT proceed past §8.1 step 4.

additional_malformed_examples_for_implem.why: empty value for `recipient` — §7.1 requires <value> to be a non-empty <bare-token> | <quoted-string> | <integer> | <url>
case violation — §7.1 / §7.2 require lowercase product and verb

soft_examples_validateScope_only.why: empty parentheses parses cleanly per §7.1 (no constraints, equivalent to `lock:seal`); only validateScope's strict registry mode might object
parses cleanly — §7.1 grammar accepts any value text. validateScope (or sub-scope evaluation per §7.4) is where the integer-required check fires. Different code path, not §8.1 step 4.

harness_assertion: Given any scope in `inputs.scopes` or `additional_malformed_examples_for_implementer_smoke_tests`, a conformant parser MUST throw at the §7.1 grammar level (E_BAD_SCOPE_GRAMMAR). The `soft_examples_validateScope_only` cases parse cleanly per §7.1 and are rejected only by the registry-aware §7.6 / §7.4 validators — implementations may surface them as E_BAD_SCOPE_GRAMMAR if they do strict-mode validation up-front, or as E_SCOPE_DENIED if they catch the violation at action-evaluation time.
